name: mongospecs

metadata.name: mongospecs
metadata.summary: simple mongo ODM with support for msgspec, pydantic, and attrs

documentation:
# mongospecs

Built on top of the https://github.com/GetmeUK/MongoFrames ODM, with:
- Pydantic (`BaseModel`), attrs (`@define`), and msgspec (`Struct`) support for defining schema models (specs)
- Type-hints
- *Spec adapters*
  - Build a spec out of existing BaseModels, attrs classes, or Structs
- *Raw BSON encode/decode for specs/mongo documents*
## Install
Choose from one of the following:
* `pip install mongospecs` for msgspec only
* `pip install mongospecs[pydantic]` for msgspec and pydantic only
* `pip install mongospecs[attrs]` for msgspec and attrs only
* `pip install mongospecs[pydantic,attrs]` for msgspec, pydantic, and attrs

### Example
```python
# Import Spec, either...
## 1. with pydantic:
from mongospecs.pydantic import Spec

## 2. with msgspec:
from mongospecs.msgspec import Spec

## 3. with attrs:
from mongospecs.attrs import Spec

# Define schema model
class Dragon(Spec):
    _collection = "dragons" # Optional. If not defined, uses the class name by default.

    name: str
    breed: Optional[str] = None

# create
burt = Dragon(name="Burt", breed="Cold-drake")
print(burt.name)  # Burt
print(burt.breed) # Cold-drake

# insert
burt.insert()
print(burt.id)  # inserted document ObjectId

# fetch
doc = Dragon.find_one({"name": "Burt"})  # returns raw mongo document

# delete
burt.delete()
```

classes.name: mongospecs.query.Q
documentation:
Start point for the query creation, the Q class is a special type of class
that's typically initialized by appending an attribute, for example:

    Q.hit_points > 100

The Group class is used as a base class for operators that group together
two or more conditions.

class_variables.name: mongospecs.pagination.Paginator.orphans
documentation:
If a value is specified for orphans then the last page will be able to
hold the additional results (up to the value of orphans). This can
help prevent users being presented with pages contain only a few
results.

class_variables.name: mongospecs.pagination.Paginator.filter_kwargs
documentation: Any additional filter arguments applied when selecting results such as sort and projection

A pagination class for slicing query results into pages. This class is
designed to work with Spec classes.

documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.All
documentation:
The All operator selects documents where the value of the field is an list
that contains all the specified elements.
Operators

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.ElemMatch
documentation:
The ElemMatch operator matches documents that contain an array field with at
least one element that matches all the specified query criteria.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.Exists
documentation:
When exists is True, Exists matches the documents that contain the field,
including documents where the field value is null. If exists is False, the
query returns only the documents that do not contain the field.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.In
documentation:
The In operator selects the documents where the value of a field equals any
value in the specified list.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.Not
documentation:
Not performs a logical NOT operation on the specified condition and selects
the documents that do not match. This includes documents that do not contain
the field.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.NotIn
documentation:
The NotIn operator selects documents where the field value is not in the
specified list or the field does not exists.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.Size
documentation:
The Size operator matches any list with the number of elements specified by
size.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.Type
documentation:
Type selects documents where the value of the field is an instance of the
specified BSON type.

functions.name: mongospecs.ops.SortBy
documentation: Convert a list of Q objects into list of sort instructions
Sorting

classes.name: mongospecs.ops.And
documentation:
And performs a logical AND operation on a list of two or more conditions and
selects the documents that satisfy all the conditions.
Groups

classes.name: mongospecs.ops.Or
documentation:
The Or operator performs a logical OR operation on a list of two or more
conditions and selects the documents that satisfy at least one of the
conditions.

classes.name: mongospecs.ops.Nor
documentation:
Nor performs a logical NOR operation on a list of one or more conditions and
selects the documents that fail all the conditions.

methods.name: mongospecs.pydantic.Spec.get_fields
documentation: note: pydantic BaseModel already has to_tuple

methods.name: mongospecs.pydantic.PydanticAdapter.__init__
documentation:
Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises [ValidationError][pydantic_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot
be validated to form a valid model.

__init__ uses __pydantic_self__ instead of the more common self for the first arg
to allow self as a field name.

functions.name: mongospecs.base.to_refs
documentation: Convert all Spec instances within the given value to Ids

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SpecBase.insert
documentation: Insert this document
Operations

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SpecBase.unset
documentation: Unset the given list of fields for this document.

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SpecBase.update
documentation:
Update this document. Optionally a specific list of fields to update can
be specified.

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SpecBase.upsert
documentation:
Update or Insert this document depending on whether it exists or not.
The presense of an `_id` value in the document is used to determine if
the document exists.

NOTE: This method is not the same as specifying the `upsert` flag when
calling MongoDB. When called for a document with an `_id` value, this
method will call the database to see if a record with that Id exists,
if not it will call `insert`, if so it will call `update`. This
operation is therefore not atomic and much slower than the equivalent
MongoDB operation (due to the extra call).

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SpecBase.delete
documentation: Delete this document

methods.name: mongospecs.base.SubSpecBase._projection_to_paths

Expand a $sub/$sub. projection to a single projection of True (if
inclusive) or a map of full paths (e.g `employee.company.tel`).
